tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_xor_eq_0_and_or_no_mask(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %xor = xor i32 %x, %y
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %xor, 0
  %and = and i32 %x, %y
  %or = or i32 %x, %y
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %and, i32 %or
  ret i32 %cond
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions comparison to constant C but doesn't explicitly cover the special case where C=0, which triggers the matchFalseVal path with OuterOpc=NoOps. This is a distinct optimization path in the code where FalseVal directly matches the inner operation without any outer operation or mask.

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_xor_eq_C_and_orC_swapped_operands(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %xor = xor i32 %x, %y
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %xor, %c
  %and = and i32 %x, %y
  %or = or i32 %x, %y
  %or1 = or i32 %and, %c
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %and, i32 %or1
  ret i32 %cond
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_xor_eq_C_and_orC_swapped_operands(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %and = and i32 %x, %y
  %or1 = or i32 %and, %c
  ret i32 %or1
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes operand order as X,Y but this test uses swapped operand order in the bitwise operations (xor %x,%y vs %y,%x). The summary doesn't explicitly mention commutativity handling for all operand permutations.
